description_long: This is a roughly rectangular, block-shaped stone with a weathered, uneven surface and muted coloration. The stone displays a natural, coarse texture with visible pitting, discoloration, and a few irregular marks, including a notable cavity or indentation on one face. Its coloration ranges from light gray to tan, with some brownish and yellowish patches, suggesting exposure to the elements. The edges and corners are rounded and softened, indicating significant wear over time. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible artificial modifications or carvings. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a park or roadside.
description: A weathered, rectangular stone block with a rough, pitted surface and muted gray-tan coloration. Features include rounded edges and a prominent cavity on one side.
description_view_0: The front face shows a distinct cavity or hole near the center, with a generally rough and uneven surface.
description_view_1: This side is mostly featureless, with a smoother, rounded surface and subtle color variations.
description_view_2: The back face displays mottled brownish patches and a more uniform, flat surface with no major indentations.
description_view_3: This side is similar to view 1, with a slightly more angular edge and consistent stone texture.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling and pitting, with some discoloration and a single prominent cavity; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Heavily weathered with rounded edges, surface pitting, and discoloration indicating prolonged exposure to natural elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural ground surfaces or contrasts with paved urban features; may accumulate dirt or debris at the base.
